tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  
</blueprint>

content.text:
Here's a curated list of top LA hotel recommendations across different neighborhoods and budgets. Let me save these for you!

---

## 🏨 LA Hotel Recommendations

### 🌟 Luxury
1. **Hotel Bel-Air** —
A gorgeous Dorchester Collection property that brings Hollywood regency into a new era, with a 12-acre oasis hidden in the winding canyons of Bel-Air.
Traveler rating: 4.7/5 on TripAdvisor.
2. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
A Dorchester Collection icon and the quintessential Los Angeles hotel, with a red carpet running up its entrance steps.
Standard rooms start at $1,145 per night.
3. **Fairmont Century Plaza** —
Rated 4.8/5 on TripAdvisor, it's one of the most popular luxury hotels near The Getty Center.
4. **The Maybourne Beverly Hills** —
Comes with a sterling pedigree (the group also operates Claridge's and The Connaught in London), with 205 redesigned guest rooms featuring mid-century furniture and collectible artworks.
5. **Ritz-Carlton Downtown LA** —

Commands panoramic views of the city, occupying the top four floors of a striking glass building among downtown LA's high-rise cluster, within strolling distance of the Crypto.com Arena and Grammy Museum.

### 🏖️ Beach/Santa Monica
6. **Casa del Mar** —
Runs $692 to $1,606 for direct beach access that only two LA hotels can claim.
7. **Shutters on the Beach** —
Rates run $600–$900 per night, slightly less expensive than Casa del Mar for comparable beachfront access.
The hotel offers complimentary beach bike rentals.

### 🏙️ Downtown LA
8. **Hotel Figueroa (Hyatt Unbound Collection)** —

One of the longest-standing hotels in Downtown LA, it exudes a warm and inviting atmosphere, with a dazzling two-year restoration inspired by its original 1926 Spanish Colonial splendor and standout pool area and dining options.
Rates run $200–$400 per night, making it one of the better values in central LA.

### 🎬 West Hollywood
9. **The London West Hollywood** —
Offers the best luxury-to-value ratio in West Hollywood; all 200 rooms measure at least 750 sq ft with private balconies, wet bars, Italian linens, and a rooftop pool with panoramic views.
10. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** *(New!)* —
A 2026 opening by Ian Schrager in the former Standard building.
Features a 16,000-square-foot open-air rooftop terrace with 360-degree views of Los Angeles.

### 💡 Key Tips
-

The trick is matching the neighborhood to your purpose: Beverly Hills for shopping/meetings, Santa Monica for beach access, Downtown LA for arts near Walt Disney Concert Hall and The Broad, and West Hollywood for restaurants and nightlife.
Hotel prices are usually on the higher side, but you can find deals by booking early.

---

Now let me save these recommendations for you!

params.title: LA Hotel Recommendations - April 2026 Trip
params.content:
Hotel recommendations for upcoming LA trip (next week from April 2, 2026):

LUXURY:
1. Hotel Bel-Air — Dorchester Collection, 12-acre oasis, 4.7/5 TripAdvisor
2. The Beverly Hills Hotel — Iconic, from ~$1,145/night
3. Fairmont Century Plaza — 4.8/5 TripAdvisor, near Getty Center
4. The Maybourne Beverly Hills — Former Montage, mid-century design, Whisky Bar
5. Ritz-Carlton Downtown LA — Top-floor panoramic views, near Crypto.com Arena

BEACH/SANTA MONICA:
6. Casa del Mar — $692–$1,606/night, direct beach access
7. Shutters on the Beach — $600–$900/night, complimentary beach bike rentals

DOWNTOWN LA:
8. Hotel Figueroa (Hyatt Unbound Collection) — $200–$400/night, 1926 Spanish Colonial, great value

WEST HOLLYWOOD:
9. The London West Hollywood — Best luxury-to-value in WeHo, all rooms 750+ sq ft, rooftop pool
10. PUBLIC West Hollywood (NEW 2026) — Ian Schrager, former Standard building, 16,000 sq ft rooftop terrace

TIPS:
- Beverly Hills = shopping/meetings
- Santa Monica = beach access
- Downtown = arts/culture (Disney Concert Hall, The Broad)
- West Hollywood = restaurants/nightlife/Sunset Strip
- Book early for better rates
